description: Trigger discrimination for the README entry-point doc (this skill) vs organizing the whole corpus (diataxis-doc-organizer), the docs pipeline (docs-as-code-architect), the contributor guide (contribution-guide-author), and new-hire onboarding (onboarding-doc-designer).

cases.prompt: Write our project's README: what it is, who it's for, a quickstart that runs, and links to the deeper docs.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — the single entry-point document with first-screen framing and a quickstart is a README. No corpus taxonomy and no toolchain is being designed.
cases.reason: Crafting the project's front-door README is this skill's core scope; it links INTO the corpus that other skills organize and build.

cases.prompt: Sort our sprawling docs into tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation, and fix the ones that are the wrong type.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — organizing a whole doc SET by Diátaxis mode is corpus organization, not the entry doc.
cases.reason: diataxis-doc-organizer shapes the whole documentation set. The README is one entry point that routes into it.

cases.prompt: Set up docs-as-code: the static-site generator, PR-based review, link-checking in CI, and versioned deploys.
cases.reason: docs-as-code-architect designs the docs toolchain/pipeline. This skill writes the README content, not the build system.

cases.prompt: Write the CONTRIBUTING guide: how to set up, branch, run tests, and get a PR reviewed and merged.
cases.reason: contribution-guide-author designs the contributor guide. The README links to it but is a broader front door for all readers.
